_metadata.description: Production-grade action verbs dataset for ATS resume analysis. Covers all industries, experience levels, and resume contexts.
sources: Harvard Office of Career Services Action Verbs Guide
Columbia University Center for Career Education
US Department of Labor O*NET Task Statements
usage_notes: verb_base is the infinitive form — use as canonical matching key
past_tense is the form most commonly found on resumes
present_tense is used for current roles
strength_score 1-5 rates ATS impact (5 = strongest signal)
ats_preferred true means this verb scores higher in ATS matching
industries array lists which sectors commonly use this verb
weak_verb_replacements lists stronger alternatives for weak verbs
regex_pattern matches all tense forms in resume text

leadership_management.description: Verbs demonstrating authority, direction, and oversight of people, teams, or organizations

verbs.example: Led a cross-functional team of 12 engineers to deliver a $2M product on time
Managed a team of 8 software engineers across 3 time zones
Directed all product strategy initiatives for a $50M business unit
Supervised daily operations of a 24-person customer support team
Oversaw quality control processes across 5 manufacturing plants
Spearheaded the company-wide digital transformation initiative
Championed adoption of agile methodologies across 4 engineering teams
Orchestrated a company-wide ERP migration involving 200+ stakeholders
Pioneered the company's first machine learning pipeline for fraud detection
Established the company's first dedicated cybersecurity operations center
Founded a SaaS startup that reached $1M ARR within 18 months
Delegated tasks and responsibilities across 3 project teams
Mentored 6 junior developers in software engineering best practices
Coached 12 sales representatives on consultative selling techniques
Mobilized 50 volunteers for a community disaster relief initiative
Chaired the monthly executive leadership committee meetings
Presided over quarterly board meetings and investor presentations
Stewarded a $5M endowment fund for a nonprofit organization
Cultivated strategic partnerships with 15 enterprise clients
Governed data privacy policies across 4 international subsidiaries
Administered network infrastructure for 500+ endpoint enterprise environment
Empowered team members to own projects independently through structured mentorship
Headed the global supply chain optimization project
Galvanized a demoralized team to exceed quarterly targets by 40%
Instituted a performance management framework adopted company-wide

achievement_improvement.description: Verbs demonstrating measurable results, performance gains, and positive impact
relevant_roles: all roles — most impactful with metrics

verbs.example: Achieved 127% of annual sales quota for 3 consecutive years
Improved system uptime from 94% to 99.9% through infrastructure redesign
Increased monthly recurring revenue by 45% within 6 months
Reduced operational costs by $2.3M annually through process automation
Saved $850K annually by renegotiating vendor contracts
Boosted website conversion rate from 2.1% to 6.8% through A/B testing
Maximized team productivity by implementing structured sprint planning
Minimized customer churn by 28% through proactive success programs
Accelerated product release cycle from 6 months to 6 weeks
Exceeded customer satisfaction targets by 22% for 4 consecutive quarters
Surpassed annual revenue goals by 35% in the first year of launch
Elevated brand awareness by 60% through targeted digital campaigns
Strengthened client retention by 40% through personalized onboarding
Transformed a legacy monolithic system into a scalable microservices architecture
Revolutionized supply chain visibility using IoT sensors and real-time analytics
Doubled the engineering team headcount while maintaining 95% retention
Tripled organic traffic through a content SEO overhaul in 9 months
Outperformed all regional competitors in customer satisfaction scores
Grew the user base from 10K to 250K in 14 months
Expanded market presence into 8 new international territories
Scaled the platform infrastructure to handle 10x traffic growth
Revitalized a stagnant product line resulting in 35% revenue recovery
Generated $4.2M in new business through strategic partnership development
Slashed processing time by 70% through intelligent workflow automation

technical_development.description: Verbs used by engineers, developers, and technical professionals

verbs.example: Developed a RESTful API serving 50M requests per day with 99.99% uptime
Built a real-time data pipeline processing 5TB of user events daily
Engineered a distributed caching system reducing database load by 80%
Architected a cloud-native microservices platform for 10M daily active users
Designed database schema supporting 99.9% query performance at scale
Implemented OAuth 2.0 authentication across 12 enterprise applications
Deployed containerized applications using Kubernetes across 3 cloud regions
Automated CI/CD pipeline reducing deployment time from 4 hours to 12 minutes
Optimized SQL queries reducing average response time from 8s to 180ms
Integrated 6 third-party payment gateways into the core transaction platform
Refactored legacy codebase reducing technical debt by 60% and bug rate by 45%
Programmed custom data validation modules in Python
verbs.weak_verb_note: Use 'Developed', 'Engineered', or 'Built' instead for stronger impact
Replace with 'Developed', 'Built', or 'Engineered' for stronger signal

verbs.example: Migrated on-premise infrastructure to AWS, reducing costs by 40%
Containerized 40+ microservices using Docker and Kubernetes
Debugged critical memory leak affecting production systems
verbs.weak_verb_note: Use 'Resolved', 'Diagnosed', or 'Identified and fixed' for stronger framing

verbs.example: Provisioned cloud infrastructure using Terraform across AWS and Azure
Configured high-availability clusters supporting 99.95% SLA
Prototyped an ML model for real-time fraud detection with 97% accuracy
Hardened Linux server configurations per CIS benchmark Level 2 standards
Modernized 15-year-old legacy COBOL system to Java Spring Boot architecture

verbs.weak_verb_note: Weak — use 'Ensured', 'Upheld', or 'Sustained' with a metric for impact
Use 'Validated', 'Verified', or 'Conducted testing' for stronger framing

verbs.example: Simulated APT attack scenarios to identify gaps in incident response playbooks
Validated ML model performance across 5 real-world datasets
Documented API specifications and system architecture for 30+ engineers
Troubleshot production incidents achieving mean time to resolution under 15 minutes
Scripted automated threat detection alerts using Python and Splunk APIs

analysis_research.description: Verbs demonstrating analytical thinking, investigation, and data-driven decision making

verbs.example: Analyzed 2TB of customer behavior data to identify conversion bottlenecks
Evaluated 12 vendor solutions against security and compliance requirements
Assessed organizational cybersecurity maturity against NIST CSF framework
Investigated 200+ security incidents identifying 3 critical zero-day vulnerabilities
Researched emerging threat vectors to update security awareness training
Identified $1.2M in annual cost-saving opportunities through process analysis
Diagnosed root cause of persistent authentication failures in 4 hours
Audited 150 vendor security questionnaires for SOC 2 compliance
Forecasted quarterly revenue with 94% accuracy using predictive ML models
Monitored network traffic across 5,000 endpoints using SIEM dashboards
Tracked 45 KPIs across 6 business units using real-time Power BI dashboards
Measured campaign ROI across 12 marketing channels monthly
Quantified business impact of security incidents to prioritize remediation
Interpreted complex clinical trial data for executive leadership decisions
Benchmarked security posture against CIS Controls and ISO 27001 standards
Synthesized findings from 3 threat intelligence sources into actionable reports
Mapped attack surfaces across 200 assets using MITRE ATT&CK framework
Surveyed 500 customers to identify key friction points in the onboarding process
Detected and contained a ransomware intrusion within 22 minutes of initial alert
Categorized 1,500 data assets by sensitivity level for GDPR compliance
Classified network threats using ML model with 96% precision score
Profiled 12 threat actor groups using OSINT and dark web intelligence
Uncovered a previously unknown lateral movement technique during red team exercise
Discovered 3 critical CVEs in proprietary software through code review

project_operations.description: Verbs for executing projects, managing timelines, and driving operational excellence

verbs.example: Executed a $3.5M infrastructure modernization program 2 months ahead of schedule
Delivered 18 features in 6 sprints with zero critical post-release bugs
Coordinated cross-functional teams across 4 departments for ERP rollout
Planned and executed office relocation affecting 200+ employees
Prioritized backlog of 80 features using value/effort scoring framework
Allocated $8M capital budget across 12 technology investment priorities
Streamlined onboarding process reducing time-to-productivity by 35%
Standardized security patch management across 600 servers
Consolidated 6 redundant vendor contracts saving $400K annually
Launched SaaS product to 5,000 beta users with 89% satisfaction score

verbs.weak_verb_note: Weak alone — add outcome: 'Completed X, resulting in Y'

verbs.example: Mitigated 45 high-severity vulnerabilities within SLA before public disclosure
Prevented estimated $4.7M in fraud losses through anomaly detection implementation
Ensured 100% PCI-DSS compliance across all payment processing systems
Formalized incident response playbooks reducing average response time by 60%
Restructured the QA process cutting defect escape rate from 12% to 2%
Overhauled the legacy billing system reducing billing errors by 95%

communication_presentation.description: Verbs demonstrating verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills

verbs.example: Presented quarterly security posture report to board of directors
Authored 3 peer-reviewed papers on adversarial machine learning
Drafted comprehensive data governance policies adopted company-wide
Published technical vulnerability disclosure followed by 50K+ reads
Briefed C-suite executives on threat landscape and risk posture weekly
Articulated technical security concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Negotiated SaaS contracts saving $1.8M over 3-year term
Persuaded executive leadership to invest $2M in zero-trust architecture
Influenced product roadmap by presenting customer data to C-suite
Advised 8 Fortune 500 clients on enterprise security architecture
Trained 300 employees on phishing awareness, reducing incidents by 68%
Educated development teams on secure coding practices and OWASP Top 10
Translated technical security findings into business risk language for executives

collaboration_partnership.description: Verbs demonstrating cross-functional teamwork, partnerships, and stakeholder engagement

verbs.example: Collaborated with legal and compliance teams to draft GDPR response procedures
Partnered with 5 cloud vendors to negotiate enterprise licensing agreements
Liaised between product and engineering teams to resolve delivery bottlenecks
Aligned security roadmap with business objectives across 4 divisions
Engaged 20+ stakeholders to gather requirements for ERP migration
Facilitated quarterly tabletop exercises for incident response teams
Brokered strategic partnership with AWS valued at $2.5M annually
Consulted 12 SME clients on cybersecurity risk assessments

strategic_planning.description: Verbs used by senior leaders and strategy professionals

verbs.example: Strategized 3-year cybersecurity maturity roadmap aligned to ISO 27001
Formulated data retention policies for GDPR and CCPA compliance
Conceptualized and launched an AI-driven threat hunting platform
Envisioned and delivered a fully automated SOC reducing analyst workload 40%
Devised a novel intrusion detection algorithm reducing false positives by 85%
Shaped the company's 5-year digital security transformation strategy
Redefined security operations model shifting from reactive to proactive threat hunting

financial_budget.description: Verbs used in finance, accounting, and budget management contexts

verbs.example: Budgeted and managed $12M annual IT security spend
Procured enterprise security tools saving $600K vs list pricing
Leveraged existing cloud infrastructure to reduce security tooling costs by 30%
Forecasted quarterly budget variances with 97% accuracy
Reconciled monthly financial reports across 6 business units
Secured $4M Series A funding to accelerate product development

compliance_security.description: Verbs specific to cybersecurity, compliance, risk management, and governance roles

verbs.example: Secured cloud environment achieving zero critical findings in external audit
Protected PII of 2M+ customers through end-to-end encryption implementation
Remediated 342 vulnerabilities across production systems within 90 days
Penetrated target network undetected using custom C2 framework
Exploited SQL injection vulnerabilities in client web applications during authorized testing
Contained active ransomware outbreak within 18 minutes limiting blast radius
Eradicated persistent malware from 400 endpoints using forensic imaging techniques
Patched 1,200 endpoints within 48 hours of critical zero-day disclosure
Triaged 150+ daily security alerts with 98% accuracy rate

verbs.weak_verb_note: Weak — use 'Achieved compliance', 'Maintained 100% compliance', or 'Ensured adherence'

verbs.example: Enforced least-privilege access controls across 3,000 user accounts
Hunted for threat actor TTPs using Sigma rules and Splunk threat intelligence
Disclosed 5 critical vulnerabilities via coordinated responsible disclosure process
Classified 5,000 data assets by sensitivity for GDPR compliance program

data_science_ml.description: Verbs specific to data science, ML engineering, and AI research roles

verbs.example: Trained BERT-based NER model achieving 94% F1 score on legal documents
Modeled customer churn probability achieving AUC of 0.92
Predicted equipment failures 7 days in advance using LSTM time-series models
Extracted structured entities from 10M unstructured documents using spaCy
Ingested 500GB daily event streams using Apache Kafka and Spark Streaming
Fine-tuned LLaMA 2 model on domain-specific cybersecurity corpus
Vectorized resume text using sentence-transformers for semantic matching

innovation_creativity.description: Verbs demonstrating creative thinking, invention, and bringing new ideas to life

verbs.example: Innovated novel threat detection approach now used as industry benchmark
Invented a patented algorithm for real-time behavioral anomaly detection
Ideated 20+ product features through structured design sprints
Created automated vulnerability scanning framework used across 30 engagements
Reimagined security onboarding process reducing time-to-compliant from 90 to 15 days
Introduced DevSecOps practices reducing security defect rate by 55%

sales_business_development.description: Verbs for sales, account management, and revenue generation roles

verbs.example: Sold $3.2M in enterprise cybersecurity solutions exceeding quota by 48%
Closed 5 enterprise deals worth $8M in combined contract value
Prospected 200+ accounts building pipeline worth $4M
Upsold 30% of existing accounts adding $1.2M ARR in year one
Retained 96% of enterprise accounts despite competitive market pressure

healthcare_clinical.description: Verbs specific to medical, nursing, and healthcare administration roles

verbs.example: Diagnosed complex neurological conditions for 30+ patients per week
Treated 40+ patients daily in high-volume emergency department
Prescribed evidence-based treatment protocols for 200+ chronic disease patients
Triaged 60+ patients daily ensuring appropriate care prioritization

education_training.description: Verbs for teaching, instructing, curriculum development, and learning roles

verbs.example: Taught advanced Python to 120 undergraduate students with 97% pass rate
Instructed security awareness workshops for 500+ employees quarterly
Facilitated hands-on cybersecurity bootcamp for 80 career-changers
Designed 40-hour penetration testing curriculum adopted by 3 universities
Developed e-learning modules reducing onboarding time by 40%
Assessed student competency through practical lab exercises and oral examinations
Mentored 15 PhD candidates through dissertation research and defense
Lectured on network security fundamentals to 200 undergraduate students
Evaluated student penetration testing reports using standardized rubrics
Tutored 20 students in Python programming and data structures weekly
Accredited 3 new cybersecurity degree programs meeting ABET standards

supply_chain_logistics.description: Verbs specific to supply chain, procurement, and logistics operations

verbs.example: Sourced 12 qualified vendors reducing component lead time by 35%
Negotiated supplier contracts saving $2.1M annually across 8 key vendors

verbs.weak_verb_note: Weak alone — quantify: 'Managed shipment of 50K units monthly'

verbs.example: Optimized warehouse layout increasing pick efficiency by 28%
Forecasted demand with 96% accuracy reducing overstock by $800K
Tracked 5,000 monthly shipments achieving 99.2% on-time delivery rate
Coordinated cross-border logistics for 50-country distribution network
Audited 40 supplier facilities for ethical sourcing compliance

hr_recruiting.description: Verbs specific to talent acquisition, HR management, and people operations

verbs.example: Recruited 45 senior engineers reducing time-to-hire by 30%
Hired and onboarded 120 employees during rapid company expansion phase
Onboarded 200 new hires achieving 95% 90-day retention rate
Evaluated 500+ candidates annually for technical and cultural fit

verbs.weak_verb_note: Weak alone — pair with outcome: 'Interviewed 300+ candidates, achieving 40% offer acceptance rate'

verbs.example: Retained top performers through structured career development programs achieving 94% retention
Restructured talent acquisition function improving quality-of-hire by 40%
Implemented flexible work policies increasing employee satisfaction by 22%
Mediated 25 workplace disputes achieving resolution without escalation

verbs.weak_verb_note: Weak — use 'Evaluated', 'Assessed', or 'Identified top candidates from pool of X'

marketing_growth.description: Verbs for marketing, demand generation, brand, and growth roles

verbs.example: Promoted product launch across 8 channels achieving 2M impressions in week one
Rebranded company identity increasing brand recognition by 45% in target market
Positioned brand as market leader in enterprise cybersecurity segment
Segmented customer base into 8 personas improving email open rates by 34%
Drove 120% increase in qualified pipeline through ABM campaigns
Targeted enterprise accounts with personalized ABM campaigns achieving 28% response rate
Optimized Google Ads campaigns reducing CPA from $185 to $62
Acquired 15,000 new subscribers in Q3 through referral program redesign
Curated weekly threat intelligence newsletter with 8,000 security professional subscribers
Activated multi-channel demand generation strategy generating 400 MQLs per month

weak_verbs_to_avoid.description: These verbs are ATS-weak and should be flagged for replacement. Use this category to identify and suggest stronger alternatives.
weak_verbs_to_avoid.usage: When detected on a resume, flag as weak and suggest replacement from strong_replacements list

verbs.weakness_reason: Vague — does not convey ownership or measurable contribution
verbs.example_weak: Helped the team with security audits
verbs.example_strong: Supported 6 security audits identifying 23 high-risk compliance gaps

verbs.weakness_reason: Extremely vague — tells nothing about contribution or impact
verbs.example_strong: Led cloud migration project moving 200 servers to AWS, reducing costs 40%

verbs.weakness_reason: Implies secondary role — use only for entry-level. Prefer ownership verbs.
verbs.example_strong: Conducted penetration testing across 15 web applications identifying 8 critical vulnerabilities

verbs.weakness_reason: Shows no leadership or ownership — just presence
verbs.example_strong: Led tabletop incident response exercises for 50-person security team quarterly

verbs.weakness_reason: Passive voice — replace entirely with active strong verb
verbs.example_weak: Was responsible for network security monitoring
verbs.example_strong: Monitored network security across 5,000 endpoints using SIEM reducing MTTD by 40%

verbs.weakness_reason: Vague and passive-sounding — does not convey expertise
verbs.example_strong: Resolved 200+ security incidents annually with average containment time of 45 minutes

verbs.weakness_reason: Tells ATS you touched a tool but not what you accomplished with it
verbs.example_weak: Used Python to analyze security logs
verbs.example_strong: Developed Python automation scripts analyzing 10M security events daily reducing analyst time 60%

verbs.weakness_reason: Extremely generic — replace with specific creation or achievement verb
verbs.example_weak: Made improvements to the security dashboard
verbs.example_strong: Redesigned security dashboard reducing analyst investigation time by 35%

verbs.weakness_reason: Weakest possible verb — tells ATS nothing
verbs.example_strong: Conducted security assessments for 25 enterprise clients identifying $3M in risk exposure

verbs.weakness_reason: Implies uncertainty and failure — remove completely
verbs.example_strong: Optimized system performance achieving 300% throughput improvement

verbs.weakness_reason: Describes input not output — replace with what you achieved after learning
verbs.example_weak: Learned how to use Splunk for threat detection
verbs.example_strong: Applied Splunk SIEM to detect and respond to 150+ threats reducing MTTD by 60%

verbs.weakness_reason: Informal and vague — replace with formal action verb
verbs.example_strong: Earned CISSP certification passing on first attempt with 850/1000 score

verbs.weakness_reason: Weak without metric — always pair with specific outcome
verbs.example_strong: Achieved 100% PCI-DSS compliance across all payment systems for 3 consecutive audits

verbs.weakness_reason: Weak without specific contribution — add what specifically you contributed
verbs.example_strong: Developed threat hunting playbooks adopted across 4-person SOC team

verbs.weakness_reason: Adjective not verb — grammatically incorrect as bullet start
verbs.example_strong: Managed security operations center handling 500+ alerts daily

verbs.weakness_reason: Passive and vague — implies minimal agency
verbs.example_weak: Was involved in a major cloud migration
verbs.example_strong: Led cloud migration for 150 applications cutting infrastructure costs by 35%

verbs.weakness_reason: Secondary role verb — acceptable for entry level only. Senior roles should use ownership verbs.
verbs.example_strong: Enabled security team efficiency gains of 40% by building automated alert correlation engine
Conducted penetration tests across 8 web applications discovering 4 critical RCE vulnerabilities

flat_verb_lookup.description: Flat lowercase list of all strong verbs for O(1) ATS detection. Load as Python set().

weak_verb_lookup.description: Flat lowercase list of weak verbs to flag during ATS analysis

verb_aliases_lookup.description: Flat alias map for fuzzy matching. Key = alias (lowercase), Value = canonical verb base.

ats_scoring_config.description: Configuration for ATS verb scoring in resume analysis

verb_density_warnings.warning_message: '{verb}' used {count} times — vary your action verbs for stronger impact

industry_verb_recommendations.description: Top 10 recommended power verbs by industry/role type
